title: Physical, Service, and Performance Troubleshooting · Mastery Check
summary: Resolve a multi-symptom campus incident across cabling, interfaces, switching, routing, addressing, wireless, and application performance. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an evidence-backed incident resolution packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition.
Interpret CRC, runts, giants, drops, errors, negotiation, and port status trends with time and traffic context.
Diagnose PoE budget or standard problems and transceiver protocol, wavelength, fiber, distance, and signal mismatches.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Net Cable Faults · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: network troubleshooting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Wireless Performance Faults applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Diagnose channel overlap, interference, signal loss, insufficient coverage, disassociation, roaming, and capacity.
